A journey that took everything — and was worth every single moment.

The Journey

Four years at UNO — in pictures

It Takes a Village

None of this would have happened without these people.

Getting here took years — a pandemic, a brain injury, a cross-country move, a new baby, a brand new career, and so much more. These people didn't just cheer from the sidelines. They got in the mud. This one's for all of you.

Ben
for supporting me emotionally, financially, and sometimes physically while I pursued my dreams — and always believing I could, even when I wasn't sure.
My Kids
who have really sacrificed the most while I got through school.
My Mom
for showing me that dreams are always worth chasing, even if you're a mom — maybe especially if you're a mom.
Amanda
for the initial pep talk that set me on the path toward this specific moment.
Kelsey
for walking alongside me as I struggled through school — and a pandemic, and a brain injury, and a move across the country, and a new baby, and a new career, and and and...
Josh & Drew
for ALWAYS having time for me, no matter what I needed to talk (or cry) to them about. I prayed for 12 years for a sibling and got way more than I ever dreamed. I wanted a playmate; I got 2 of my best friends.
Dad
for my work ethic and love of good music and a good dad joke.
Grandma Katrina
for many hours spent on a porch or the phone solving all the world's problems.
Northlake UUC
for being my found family in a new city and fostering our family through an intensely hard transition.
Averi, Jamie, Morgan & Crystal
for being the last push to get me through. My first set of friends in Seattle who wanted nothing more than to see me succeed and helped me transition into a brand new career.

Joining From Afar

You are so loved — here's how to celebrate from wherever you are

To everyone who couldn't make the trip —
Know that your love and support got Lacy here just as much as anyone in that room. This page is for you. Tune in, send some love, and celebrate along on May 8th. It means everything.

Watch the Graduation Live

The ceremony begins at 3:00 PM CT on Friday, May 8, 2026 at Baxter Arena, Omaha.
UNO will stream it live — link posted here as soon as it's available.

UNO Commencement Info →

3:00 PM CTGraduation ceremony begins at Baxter Arena — tune in!

7:00 PM CTGraduation dinner at Gorat's Steakhouse
Sat 6 PM CTGraduation party at the AirBnB
